sections.label: Section 1.1
sections.original: Αἰδεῖσθε ξενίων κεχρημένον ἠδὲ δόμοιο ,
sections.translation: Have [*] reverence for him who needs a home and stranger ’ s dole ,

sections.label: Section 1.2
sections.original: οἳ πόλιν αἰπεινήν , Κύμην ἐριώπιδα κούρην ,
sections.translation: all ye who dwell in the high city of Cyme , the lovely maiden ,

sections.label: Section 1.3
sections.original: ναίετε , Σαρδήνης πόδα νείατον ὑψικόμοιο ,
sections.translation: hard by the foothills of lofty Sardene ,

sections.label: Section 1.4
sections.original: ἀμβρόσιον πίνοντες ὕδωρ θείου ποταμοῖο ,
sections.translation: ye who drink the heavenly water of the divine stream ,

sections.label: Section 1.5
sections.original: Ἕρμου δινήεντος , ὃν ἀθάνατος τέκετο Ζεύς .
sections.translation: eddying Hermus , whom deathless Zeus begot .

sections.label: Section 2.1
sections.original: Αἶψα πόδες με φέροιεν ἐς αἰδοίων πόλιν ἀνδρῶν ·
sections.translation: Speedily may my feet bear me to some town of righteous men ;

sections.label: Section 2.2
sections.original: τῶν γὰρ καὶ θυμὸς πρόφρων καὶ μῆτις ἀρίστη .
sections.translation: for their hearts are generous and their wit is best .

sections.label: Section 3.1
sections.original: Χαλκέη παρθένος εἰμί , Μίδεω δ ’ ἐπὶ σήματι κεῖμαι ·
sections.translation: I am a maiden of bronze and am set upon the tomb of Midas .

sections.label: Section 3.2
sections.original: ἔστ ’ ἂν ὕδωρ τε νάῃ [*] καὶ δένδρεα μακρὰ τεθήλῃ ,
sections.translation: While the waters flow and tall trees flourish ,

sections.label: Section 3.3
sections.original: ἠέλιος τ ’ ἀνιὼν λάμπῃ λαμπρά τε σελήνη ,
sections.translation: and the sun rises and shines and the bright moon also ;

sections.label: Section 3.4
sections.original: καὶ ποταμοί γε ῥέωσιν ἀνακλύζῃ δὲ θάλασσα ,
sections.translation: while rivers run and the sea breaks on the shore ,

sections.label: Section 3.5
sections.original: αὐτοῦ τῇδε μένουσα πολυκλαύτου ἐπὶ τύμβου
sections.translation: ever remaining on this mournful tomb ,

sections.label: Section 3.6
sections.original: ἀγγελέω παριοῦσι , Μίδης ὅτι τῇδε τέθαπται .
sections.translation: I tell the passer-by that Midas here lies buried .

sections.label: Section 4.1
sections.original: Οἵῃ μ ’ αἴσῃ δῶκε πατὴρ Ζεὺς κύρμα γενέσθαι ,
sections.translation: To what a fate did Zeus the Father give me a prey

sections.label: Section 4.2
sections.original: νήπιον αἰδοίης ἐπὶ γούνασι μητρὸς ἀτάλλων .
sections.translation: even while he made me to grow , a babe at my mother ’ s knees !

sections.label: Section 4.3
sections.original: ἥν ποτ ’ ἐπύργωσαν βουλῇ Διὸς αἰγιόχοιο
sections.translation: By the will of Zeus who holds the aegis

sections.label: Section 4.4
sections.original: λαοὶ Φρίκωνος , μάργων ἐπιβήτορες ἵππων ,
sections.translation: the people of Phricon , riders on wanton horses ,

sections.label: Section 4.5
sections.original: ὁπλότεροι μαλεροῖο πυρὸς κρίνοντες Ἄρηα ,
sections.translation: more active than raging fire in the test of war ,

sections.label: Section 4.6
sections.original: Αἰολίδα Σμύρνην ἁλιγείτονα , ποντοτίνακτον ,
sections.translation: once built the towers of Aeolian Smyrna , wave-shaken neighbor to the sea ,

sections.label: Section 4.7
sections.original: ἥντε δι ’ ἀγλαὸν εἶσιν ὕδωρ ἱεροῖο Μέλητος ·
sections.translation: through which glides the pleasant stream of sacred Meles ;

sections.label: Section 4.8
sections.original: ἔνθεν ἀπορνύμεναι κοῦραι Διός , ἀγλαὰ τέκνα .
sections.translation: thence [*] arose the daughters of Zeus , glorious children ,

sections.label: Section 4.9
sections.original: ἠθελέτην κλῇσαι δῖαν χθόνα καὶ πόλιν ἀνδρῶν .
sections.translation: and would fain have made famous that fair country and the city of its people .

sections.label: Section 4.10
sections.original: οἳ δ ’ ἀπανηνάσθην ἱερὴν ὄπα , φῆμιν ἀοιδῆς ,
sections.translation: But in their folly those men scorned the divine voice and renown of song ,

sections.label: Section 4.11
sections.original: ἀφραδίῃ · τῶν μέν τε παθών τις φράσσεται αὖτις ,
sections.translation: and in trouble shall one of them remember this hereafter —

sections.label: Section 4.12
sections.original: ὅς σφιν ὀνείδεσσιν τὸν ἐμὸν διεμήσατο πότμον .
sections.translation: he who with scornful words to them [*] contrived my fate .

sections.label: Section 4.13
sections.original: κῆρα δ ’ ἐγώ , τήν μοι θεὸς ὤπασε γεινομένῳ περ ,
sections.translation: Yet I will endure the lot which heaven gave me even at my birth ,

sections.label: Section 4.14
sections.original: τλήσομαι ἀκράαντα φέρων τετληότι θυμῷ ·
sections.translation: bearing my disappointment with a patient heart .

sections.label: Section 4.15
sections.original: οὐδέ τί μοι φίλα γυῖα μένειν ἱεραῖς ἐν ἀγυιαῖς
sections.translation: My dear limbs yearn not to stay in the sacred streets

sections.label: Section 4.16
sections.original: Κύμης ὁρμαίνουσι , μέγας δέ με θυμὸς ἐπείγει
sections.translation: of Cyme , but rather my great heart urges me

sections.label: Section 4.17
sections.original: δῆμον ἐς ἀλλοδαπῶν ἰέναι , ὀλίγον περ ἐόντα .
sections.translation: to go unto another country , small though I am .

sections.label: Section 5.1
sections.original: Θεστορίδη , θνητοῖσιν ἀνωίστων πολέων περ ,
sections.translation: Thestorides , full many things there are that mortals cannot sound ;

sections.label: Section 5.2
sections.original: οὐδὲν ἀφραστότερον πέλεται νόου ἀνθρώποισιν .
sections.translation: but there is nothing more unfathomable than the heart of man .

sections.label: Section 6.1
sections.original: Κλῦθι , Ποσείδαον , μεγαλοσθενές , ἐννοσίγαιε ,
sections.translation: Hear me , Poseidon , strong shaker of the earth ,

sections.label: Section 6.2
sections.original: εὐρυχόρου μεδέων ἠδὲ ξανθοῦ Ἑλικῶνος ,
sections.translation: ruler of wide-spread , tawny Helicon !

sections.label: Section 6.3
sections.original: δὸς δ ’ οὖρον καλὸν καὶ ἀπήμονα νόστον ἰδέσθαι
sections.translation: Give a fair wind and sight of safe return

sections.label: Section 6.4
sections.original: ναύταις , οἳ νηὸς πομποὶ ἠδ ’ ἀρχοὶ ἔασι ·
sections.translation: to the shipmen who speed and govern this ship .

sections.label: Section 6.5
sections.original: δὸς δ ’ ἐς ὑπωρείην ὑψικρήμνοιο Μίμαντος
sections.translation: And grant that when I come to the nether slopes of towering Mimas

sections.label: Section 6.6
sections.original: αἰδοίων μ ’ ἐλθόντα βροτῶν ὁσίων τε κυρῆσαι ,
sections.translation: I may find honorable , god-fearing men .

sections.label: Section 6.7
sections.original: φῶτά τε τισαίμην , ὃς ἐμὸν νόον ἠπεροπεύσας
sections.translation: Also may I avenge me on the wretch who deceived me

sections.label: Section 6.8
sections.original: ὠδύσατο Ζῆνα ξένιον ξενίην τε τράπεζαν .
sections.translation: and grieved Zeus the lord of guests and his own guest-table .

sections.label: Section 7.1
sections.original: Πότνια Γῆ , πάνδωρε , δότειρα μελίφρονος ὄλβου ,
sections.translation: Queen Earth , all bounteous giver of honey-hearted wealth ,

sections.label: Section 7.2
sections.original: ὡς ἄρα δὴ τοῖς μὲν φωτῶν εὔοχθος ἐτύχθης ,
sections.translation: how kindly , it seems , you are to some ,

sections.label: Section 7.3
sections.original: τοῖσι δὲ δύσβωλος καὶ τρηχεῖ , οἷς ἐχολώθης .
sections.translation: and how intractable and rough for those with whom you are angry .

sections.label: Section 8.1
sections.original: Ναῦται ποντοπόροι , στυγερῇ ἐναλίγκιοι ἄτῃ
sections.translation: Sailors , who rove the seas and whom a hateful fate has made as

sections.label: Section 8.2
sections.original: πτωκάσιν αἰθυίῃσι , βίον δύσζηλον ἔχοντες ,
sections.translation: the shy sea-fowl , living an unenviable life ,

sections.label: Section 8.3
sections.original: αἰδεῖσθε ξενίοιο Διὸς σέβας ὑψιμέδοντος ·
sections.translation: observe the reverence due to Zeus who rules on high , the god of strangers ;

sections.label: Section 8.4
sections.original: δεινὴ γὰρ μέτ ’ ὄπις ξενίου Διός , ὅς κ ’ ἀλίτηται .
sections.translation: for terrible is the vengeance of this god afterwards for whosoever has sinned .

sections.label: Section 9.1
sections.original: [*] Ὑμέας , ὦ ξεῖνοι , ἄνεμος λάβεν ἀντίος ἐλθών ·
sections.translation: Strangers , a contrary wind has caught you :

sections.label: Section 9.2
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ ἐμὲ νῦν δέξασθε , καὶ ὁ πλόος ἔσσεται ὑμῖν .
sections.translation: but even now take me aboard and you shall make your voyage .

sections.label: Section 10.1
sections.original: Ἄλλη τίς σευ πεύκη ἀμείνονα καρπὸν ἵησιν
sections.translation: Another sort of pine shall bear a better fruit [*]

sections.label: Section 10.2
sections.original: Ἴδης ἐν κορυφῇσι πολυπτύχου ἠνεμοέσσης ,
sections.translation: than you upon the heights of furrowed , windy Ida .

sections.label: Section 10.3
sections.original: ἔνθα σίδηρος Ἄρηος ἐπιχθονίοισι βροτοῖσιν
sections.translation: For there shall mortal men get the iron that Ares loves

sections.label: Section 10.4
sections.original: ἔσσεται , εὖτ ’ ἄν μιν Κεβρήνιοι ἄνδρες ἔχωσι .
sections.translation: so soon as the Cebrenians shall hold the land .

sections.label: Section 11.1
sections.original: Γλαῦκε , βοτῶν [*] ἐπίοπτα , ἔπος τί τοι ἐν φρεσὶ θήσω ·
sections.translation: Glaucus , watchman of flocks , a word will I put in your heart .

sections.label: Section 11.2
sections.original: πρῶτον μὲν κυσὶ δεῖπνον ἐπ ’ αὐλείῃσι θύρησι
sections.translation: First give the dogs their dinner at the courtyard gate ,

sections.label: Section 11.3
sections.original: δοῦναι · τὼς γὰρ ἄμεινον · ὃ γὰρ καὶ πρῶτον ἀκούει
sections.translation: for this is well . The dog first hears

sections.label: Section 11.4
sections.original: ἀνδρὸς ἐπερχομένου καὶ ἐς ἕρκεα θηρὸς ἰόντος .
sections.translation: a man approaching and the wild-beast coming to the fence .

sections.label: Section 12.1
sections.original: Κλῦθί μευ εὐχομένου , Κουροτρόφε , δὸς δὲ γυναῖκα
sections.translation: Goddess-nurse of the young , [*] give ear to my prayer , and grant that this woman

sections.label: Section 12.2
sections.original: τήνδε νέων μὲν ἀναίνεσθαι φιλότητα καὶ εὐνήν ·
sections.translation: may reject the love-embrace of youth

sections.label: Section 12.3
sections.original: ἣ δ ’ ἐπιτερπέσθω πολιοκροτάφοισι γέρουσιν ,
sections.translation: and dote on grey-haired old men
